title: hieracium scorzonerifolium subsp. pantotrichum
intro: Hieracium scorzonerifolium subsp. pantotrichum, also known as Hawkweed, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the dandelion genus. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial effects. The plant is believed to provide health benefits such as wound healing, reduced inflammation, and improved immune function due to its bioactive compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids. Hawkweed can be prepared as a herbal remedy through tea infusions, tinctures, and topical applications, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human use.
